# KEALEE PLATFORM — Marketing Channels
## Permits & Inspections (m-permits) + Ops Services (m-ops-services)

> Focused channel strategy for the DC/MD/VA corridor with national scaling path.
> Integrates with existing GoHighLevel + Apollo.io infrastructure.

## CHANNEL 1: Google Search (Pay-Per-Click)

**Why it works:** Contractors searching for permit help or ops support have immediate intent. They're actively solving a problem right now.

### Campaign Structure

**m-permits campaigns:**

| Campaign | Keywords | Landing Page | Budget |
|----------|----------|-------------|--------|
| Permit Filing DC | "dc building permit service", "dc permit expediter", "building permit help dc" | permits.kealee.com/dc | $50/day |
| Permit Filing MD | "maryland building permit", "md permit application help", "baltimore permit service" | permits.kealee.com/md | $50/day |
| Permit Filing VA | "virginia building permit", "nova permit expediter", "fairfax permit help" | permits.kealee.com/va | $40/day |
| Permit Problems | "permit rejected resubmit", "building permit denied help", "permit revision service" | permits.kealee.com/fix | $30/day |
| Permit Software | "permit tracking software", "permit management construction", "online permit application" | permits.kealee.com/software | $40/day |

**m-ops-services campaigns:**

| Campaign | Keywords | Landing Page | Budget |
|----------|----------|-------------|--------|
| Inspection Help | "construction inspection scheduling", "inspection coordination service", "pre-inspection checklist" | ops.kealee.com/inspections | $30/day |
| Safety Compliance | "OSHA compliance contractor", "construction safety program setup", "safety documentation service" | ops.kealee.com/safety | $30/day |
| Project Admin | "construction daily report service", "change order management", "construction document management" | ops.kealee.com/admin | $25/day |
| Contractor Ops | "construction operations support", "contractor back office help", "construction admin services" | ops.kealee.com | $25/day |

**Total Google Ads budget: ~$320/day = $9,600/month**
**Target CPA:** $45–$80 for one-time products, $120–$200 for subscriptions
**Expected leads:** 150–250/month

### Implementation Steps
1. Set up Google Ads account with separate campaigns per service/jurisdiction
2. Create jurisdiction-specific landing pages (DC, MD, VA variants)
3. Install conversion tracking on all form submissions and Stripe checkouts
4. Connect Google Ads to GoHighLevel via Zapier for instant lead routing
5. Start with $150/day, scale winners after 2 weeks of data

---

## CHANNEL 2: Google Local Service Ads (LSA)

**Why it works:** LSAs appear above regular search ads with a "Google Guaranteed" badge. Contractors searching locally see you first, and you only pay per lead, not per click.

### Setup

| Detail | m-permits | m-ops-services |
|--------|-----------|----------------|
| Business Category | Permit Expediting, Construction Consulting | Construction Management, Safety Consulting |
| Service Areas | DC, Montgomery County, Prince George's County, Baltimore City, Baltimore County, Fairfax County, Arlington, Alexandria | Same |
| Budget | $75–$150/week per jurisdiction | $50–$100/week per jurisdiction |
| Lead Cost | $25–$60/lead | $20–$50/lead |

### Steps
1. Register at ads.google.com/local-services-ads
2. Complete background check and license verification (use Kealee Construction LLC credentials)
3. Set up separate profiles for permit services and operations services
4. Request Google Guaranteed badge
5. Route incoming calls/messages to GoHighLevel via call tracking number

---

## CHANNEL 3: LinkedIn Organic + Ads

**Why it works:** GCs, PMs, and construction company owners are active on LinkedIn. It's the #1 B2B platform for reaching decision-makers in construction.

### Organic Strategy (Free)

**Post 3–5x/week on Tim's personal profile + Kealee company page:**

| Day | Content Type | Example Topics |
|-----|-------------|----------------|
| Mon | Pain Point Post | "40% of permit applications in DC get rejected on first submission. Here's the #1 reason why..." |
| Tue | Case Study / Win | "We saved a GC in Bethesda 6 weeks on their permit timeline. Here's how..." |
| Wed | Quick Tip | "3 documents every DC contractor forgets to include in their permit package" |
| Thu | Industry News + Take | "New MD building code update — here's what it means for your next project" |
| Fri | CTA / Offer | "We're offering free permit audits for DC/MD/VA contractors this month. DM me." |

**LinkedIn content rules:**
- First line must be a hook (question, stat, or bold claim)
- Include 3–5 relevant hashtags: #ConstructionManagement #DCContractor #BuildingPermits #GeneralContractor #OSHA
- Tag relevant people/companies when possible
- Reply to every comment within 2 hours
- DM anyone who engages meaningfully

### LinkedIn Ads (Paid)

| Campaign | Audience | Format | Budget | CTA |
|----------|----------|--------|--------|-----|
| Permit Services | GCs, Construction Managers, PMs in DC/MD/VA | Single Image + Lead Form | $1,500/mo | "Get Your Free Permit Checklist" |
| Ops Services | Construction company owners, Ops Managers in DC/MD/VA | Carousel (show all 11 services) | $1,500/mo | "See Which Ops Service Fits You" |
| Retargeting | Website visitors (permits.kealee.com, ops.kealee.com) | Message Ad | $500/mo | "Still need help with that permit?" |

**LinkedIn audience filters:**
- Job titles: General Contractor, Construction Manager, Project Manager, Operations Manager, Owner, Superintendent
- Company size: 2–200 employees
- Industries: Construction, Building Construction, Civil Engineering
- Locations: DC metro, Baltimore metro, Northern Virginia

**Total LinkedIn Ads budget: $3,500/month**
**Expected leads: 30–60/month**

---

## CHANNEL 4: Apollo.io Cold Outreach

**Why it works:** You already have Apollo set up. Direct outreach to licensed contractors in MD/VA/DC puts your services in front of the exact buyers, and the permit pain point gives you a natural conversation opener.

### Apollo Sequence: m-permits

**Sequence name:** Permit-Pain-DC-MD-VA
**Target list:** Licensed GCs in DC/MD/VA with 2–50 employees

| Step | Day | Channel | Subject / Message |
|------|-----|---------|-------------------|
| 1 | Day 0 | Email | "Quick question about your DC permit process" — Lead with the 40% rejection rate stat, offer free permit audit |
| 2 | Day 2 | LinkedIn Connect | Personalized connection request referencing their company |
| 3 | Day 4 | Email | "Here's what's causing most DC permit rejections" — Share a short tip, link to Permit Prep Kit |
| 4 | Day 7 | LinkedIn Message | Short message: "Saw you handle projects in [jurisdiction]. We help GCs cut permit timelines in half." |
| 5 | Day 10 | Email | "Case study: [similar contractor] saved 6 weeks" — Social proof + CTA to book call |
| 6 | Day 14 | Email | "Last one from me" — Final value prop + direct link to Permit Prep Kit purchase |

### Apollo Sequence: m-ops-services

**Sequence name:** Ops-Services-DC-MD-VA
**Target list:** GCs with 5–100 employees, no dedicated back-office staff

| Step | Day | Channel | Subject / Message |
|------|-----|---------|-------------------|
| 1 | Day 0 | Email | "The hidden cost of running your own back office" — Lead with stat: avg GC spends 15hrs/week on admin |
| 2 | Day 3 | Email | "Which of these 11 things is eating your time?" — List the ops services, ask which resonates |
| 3 | Day 5 | LinkedIn Connect | Personalized request |
| 4 | Day 8 | Email | "Your inspection coordination is costing you $2K/week" — Focus on one high-pain service |
| 5 | Day 12 | LinkedIn Message | Short: "We handle the admin so you can focus on building. Worth 5 minutes?" |
| 6 | Day 15 | Email | Break-up email with link to ops.kealee.com |

### Apollo Volume

| Metric | Monthly Target |
|--------|---------------|
| New contacts added | 2,000–3,000 |
| Emails sent | 8,000–12,000 |
| LinkedIn connections | 500–800 |
| Expected reply rate | 8–15% |
| Expected meetings booked | 40–80 |
| Expected conversions | 15–30 |

---

## CHANNEL 5: GoHighLevel Automation Funnels

**Why it works:** You already have GHL set up with Christine. Use it as the central hub that receives leads from all other channels and nurtures them automatically.

### Funnel 1: Permit Lead Magnet Funnel

**Lead magnet:** "DC/MD/VA Permit Checklist — The 27 Documents You Need Before Applying"
**Landing page:** Built in GHL, embedded on permits.kealee.com/checklist
**Flow:**
1. Contractor downloads free checklist (captures name, email, phone, jurisdiction)
2. GHL tags contact: `permit-lead`, `[jurisdiction]`
3. Instant SMS: "Here's your checklist! Quick question — do you have a permit application coming up?"
4. Day 1 email: Checklist delivery + "Most GCs miss items 12–15"
5. Day 3 email: Case study of a permit approval Kealee helped with
6. Day 5 SMS: "Still working on that permit? We offer a free 15-min audit"
7. Day 7 email: Permit Prep Kit offer ($295) with urgency
8. Day 10: If no conversion → move to long-term nurture (monthly permit tips newsletter)

### Funnel 2: Ops Services Assessment

**Lead magnet:** "Construction Ops Scorecard — Rate Your Back Office in 2 Minutes"
**Landing page:** Built in GHL, embedded on ops.kealee.com/scorecard
**Flow:**
1. Contractor completes 10-question scorecard (captures pain points + contact info)
2. GHL tags based on answers: `needs-safety`, `needs-inspections`, `needs-admin`, etc.
3. Instant SMS: "Your ops score is [X]/100. Here's where you're losing time."
4. Day 1 email: Personalized results + the 1–2 services that match their weakest scores
5. Day 3 email: ROI calculator — "You're spending ~$X/month on [pain point]. We do it for $Y."
6. Day 5 SMS: "Book a 15-min call and I'll show you exactly where to start"
7. Day 7 email: Direct purchase link for their top-matched service
8. Day 14: Move to nurture sequence with weekly ops tips

### GHL Pipeline Stages

| Stage | Trigger | Next Action |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| New Lead | Form submission / import | Auto-SMS + email sequence starts |
| Engaged | Replied to SMS or email | Assign to Tim or Christine for personal follow-up |
| Discovery Call Booked | Calendly/GHL booking | Send confirmation + prep checklist |
| Proposal Sent | After call | Send Stripe payment link for matched service |
| Won | Payment received | Onboarding sequence starts |
| Lost | No response after 21 days | Move to monthly nurture newsletter |
| Nurture | Long-term | Monthly permit tips + ops insights emails |

## CHANNEL 6: Construction Industry Platforms

**Why it works:** These are where contractors already hang out, search for services, and make buying decisions.

| Platform | Strategy | Cost | Expected Leads/mo |
|----------|----------|------|-------------------|
| **Houzz Pro** | Create Kealee profile as "Permit Expediter" + "Construction Consultant". Respond to project inquiries. Run Houzz ads targeting DC/MD/VA homeowners needing permits. | $300–$600/mo | 10–25 |
| **Angi (Angie's List)** | List under "Permit Services" and "Construction Management" for all DC/MD/VA zip codes. | $200–$400/mo | 5–15 |
| **Thumbtack** | List permit filing and inspection services. Respond to instant-match requests. | Pay-per-lead ($15–$40) | 10–20 |
| **BuildingConnected** | Create vendor profile. Bid on permit coordination and ops support subcontracts from larger GCs. | Free to $99/mo | 5–10 |
| **iSqFt / ConstructConnect** | Monitor plan rooms for projects needing permit support. Reach out to GCs on active bids. | $200–$500/mo | 5–15 |
| **Procore Marketplace** | Apply to list as a Procore integration partner. Target Procore users who need permit and ops add-ons. | Free listing | 3–8 |

---

## CHANNEL 7: Local & Industry Associations

**Why it works:** Face-to-face networking with contractors who have immediate needs. Warm leads that close faster than cold outreach.

| Association | Location | Action | Cost |
|-------------|----------|--------|------|
| **AGC (Associated General Contractors)** — MD, VA, DC chapters | Regional | Join as associate member. Attend monthly meetings. Sponsor one event/quarter. | $500–$1,500/yr membership + $500–$2,000/event sponsorship |
| **NAHB (National Assoc. of Home Builders)** — local chapters | DC/MD/VA | Join HBA of Maryland. Attend Builder Marts. Set up booth at home show. | $400–$800/yr + $500–$1,500/event |
| **ABC (Associated Builders & Contractors)** — Chesapeake chapter | Baltimore/DC | Attend safety training events (perfect for ops services). Sponsor safety award. | $500–$1,200/yr |
| **MBIA (Maryland Building Industry Assoc.)** | Maryland | Join. Attend permit-related policy meetings. Offer free workshops on permit process. | $300–$600/yr |
| **NVCBIA (Northern Virginia Building Industry Assoc.)** | NoVA | Same as MBIA strategy for Virginia market. | $300–$600/yr |
| **DC Building Industry Assoc. (DCBIA)** | DC | Closest to your DC permit expertise. Board involvement if possible. | $500–$1,000/yr |
| **SMPS (Society for Marketing Professional Services)** | Regional | Network with other construction marketers. Partnership opportunities. | $300–$500/yr |

### Event Strategy
- Bring "Free Permit Audit" sign-up sheets to every event
- Hand out Permit Prep Kit samples (1-page jurisdiction checklist)
- Collect business cards → import to Apollo + GHL same day
- Follow up within 24 hours with personalized email

---

## CHANNEL 8: Content Marketing & SEO

**Why it works:** Contractors Google their problems before they Google solutions. Ranking for permit and ops pain points drives free, high-intent traffic indefinitely.

### Blog / Resource Content Calendar

| Week | m-permits Topic | m-ops-services Topic |
|------|----------------|---------------------|
| 1 | "How to Get a Building Permit in DC: Step-by-Step Guide" | "The Real Cost of Running Your Own Construction Back Office" |
| 2 | "Top 10 Reasons DC Building Permits Get Rejected" | "OSHA Compliance Checklist Every Contractor Needs" |
| 3 | "Maryland vs. Virginia vs. DC: Permit Process Comparison" | "How to Set Up Daily Reporting That Actually Gets Done" |
| 4 | "How Long Does a Building Permit Take in [County]?" | "Change Order Management: Stop Losing Money on Untracked COs" |
| 5 | "Permit Expediting: DIY vs. Hiring a Service" | "Inspection Scheduling: Why GCs Fail and How to Fix It" |
| 6 | "New 2026 Building Code Changes in MD/VA/DC" | "Lien Waiver Best Practices for Subcontractors" |
| 7 | "Commercial vs. Residential Permits: What's Different?" | "Project Closeout Checklist: 15 Items GCs Always Miss" |
| 8 | "How to Appeal a Denied Building Permit" | "Pre-Construction Admin: Set Up Your Project Right" |

### SEO Targets (Keyword → Monthly Search Volume)

| Keyword | Volume | Difficulty | Target Page |
|---------|--------|------------|-------------|
| "building permit dc" | 1,200 | Medium | permits.kealee.com/dc |
| "how to get building permit maryland" | 800 | Low | permits.kealee.com/md |
| "permit expediter dc" | 300 | Low | permits.kealee.com |
| "construction daily report template" | 2,400 | Medium | ops.kealee.com/blog/daily-reports |
| "change order template construction" | 1,800 | Medium | ops.kealee.com/blog/change-orders |
| "OSHA compliance checklist" | 3,200 | High | ops.kealee.com/blog/osha-checklist |
| "construction inspection checklist" | 1,500 | Medium | ops.kealee.com/blog/inspections |
| "lien waiver template" | 2,000 | Medium | ops.kealee.com/blog/lien-waivers |

### Content Distribution
- Publish on kealee.com/blog
- Repurpose as LinkedIn posts (excerpts with link)
- Email to GHL nurture list monthly
- Create downloadable PDF versions as lead magnets

## CHANNEL 9: YouTube & Video

**Why it works:** Construction professionals watch how-to videos. Permit walkthroughs and ops tutorials build trust and demonstrate expertise before a contractor ever contacts you.

### Video Content Plan

| Video | Length | Purpose | CTA |
|-------|--------|---------|-----|
| "How to File a DC Building Permit (Full Walkthrough)" | 8–12 min | SEO + authority | Link to Permit Prep Kit |
| "5 Permit Mistakes That Cost Contractors Thousands" | 5–7 min | Pain agitation | Link to permits.kealee.com |
| "OSHA Inspection? Here's What You Need Ready" | 5–7 min | Fear-based urgency | Link to Safety Compliance Setup |
| "I Handle Your Construction Admin So You Can Build" | 3–5 min | Brand intro / explainer | Link to ops.kealee.com |
| "Day in the Life: Construction Ops Manager" | 5–8 min | Relatability | Subscribe + follow |
| "Permit Filing Service vs. DIY: Real Cost Comparison" | 6–8 min | Conversion | Book a call CTA |
| "How We Saved a GC $47K on One Project" | 4–6 min | Case study / social proof | Link to Package A |

### YouTube SEO
- Title format: "[Pain Point] — [Solution] for [Audience]"
- Include keyword in first 3 words of title
- 2–3 paragraph descriptions with links to relevant Kealee pages
- Add timestamps for longer videos
- Pin comment with direct link to related product/service
- Cross-post short clips to LinkedIn + Instagram Reels

---

## CHANNEL 10: Referral & Partner Program

**Why it works:** Contractors trust other contractors. A referral from a peer converts 4–5x higher than cold outreach.

### Referral Program Structure

| Referrer Type | Reward | Terms |
|---------------|--------|-------|
| Existing client | 15% of first purchase or first month's subscription | Paid after referred client completes payment |
| Industry partner (architect, engineer, lender) | 10% recurring commission for 12 months | For subscription referrals only |
| Association member | $50 credit per qualified referral | Referral must book a discovery call |

### Strategic Partners to Pursue

| Partner Type | Why They Refer to You | What You Offer Them |
|-------------|----------------------|---------------------|
| Architects | Their GC clients ask about permits | You handle permits so the architect's project moves faster |
| Real estate attorneys | Clients buying properties need permits for renovations | Referral fee + priority service |
| Mortgage lenders | Construction loan borrowers need permit documentation | Co-branded permit checklist |
| Insurance agents | Contractors need safety programs for better rates | Referral to safety compliance setup |
| Accounting firms | Their contractor clients need 1099 help and ops structure | Referral fee + ops services |
| Title companies | Lien waiver processing is a natural handoff | Bulk pricing on lien waiver service |

### Implementation
1. Create referral landing page: kealee.com/refer
2. Build referral tracking in GHL (unique referral codes per partner)
3. Set up automated referral reward notifications
4. Reach out to 20 potential partners in Month 1
5. Target: 5 active referral partners sending 2–5 leads/month each by Month 3

---

## CHANNEL 11: Email Newsletter

**Why it works:** A monthly newsletter to your growing GHL contact list keeps Kealee top-of-mind for contractors who aren't ready to buy today but will be when their next project starts.

### Newsletter: "The Permit Wire" (m-permits focus)

**Frequency:** Monthly
**Audience:** All permit-related leads + existing clients
**Sections:**
1. Jurisdiction Update — Any new code changes, fee changes, or process changes in DC/MD/VA
2. Tip of the Month — One actionable permit tip
3. Case Study — One recent Kealee permit win
4. Product Spotlight — Feature one permit package or product
5. CTA — "Need help with your next permit? Book a free audit."

### Newsletter: "Ops Insider" (m-ops-services focus)

**Frequency:** Monthly
**Audience:** All ops-related leads + existing clients
**Sections:**
1. Admin Hack — One time-saving operations tip
2. Template Download — Free downloadable template (daily log, change order form, etc.)
3. ROI Spotlight — "This service saved [client] $X"
4. Compliance Alert — Any OSHA, insurance, or regulatory updates
5. CTA — "Which ops service would save you the most time? Take our 2-min quiz."

---

## CHANNEL 12: Paid Social (Facebook/Instagram)

**Why it works:** Many small-to-mid GCs and subs are on Facebook, especially in trade groups. Instagram reaches younger contractors and remodelers.

### Facebook Strategy

| Campaign | Audience | Ad Format | Budget | Offer |
|----------|----------|-----------|--------|-------|
| Permit Awareness | GCs + construction workers in DC/MD/VA, age 28–60 | Video (permit walkthrough clip) | $1,000/mo | Free Permit Checklist download |
| Ops Services | Construction business owners in DC/MD/VA | Carousel (show 11 services) | $800/mo | Free Ops Scorecard quiz |
| Retargeting | Website visitors in last 30 days | Single image | $500/mo | "Still need help? Book a free call" |

**Facebook Groups to join and post in (organically):**
- "DC Area Contractors"
- "Maryland General Contractors"
- "Northern Virginia Builders & Remodelers"
- "Construction Business Owners"
- "Subcontractors Network"

**Rules:** Add value first. Answer permit/ops questions. Mention Kealee only when directly relevant.

**Total paid social budget: $2,300/month**

| Content/SEO | $0–$500 (tools) | 20–50 (organic, grows over time) | $0–$25 |
| YouTube | $0–$300 (equipment) | 10–30 (organic, grows over time) | $0–$30 |
| Referral Program | $0 (pay on conversion) | 10–25 | $0 upfront |

## 90-Day Launch Priority

### Month 1: Foundation

- [ ] Launch Google Search Ads for top 5 permit keywords (DC focus first)
- [ ] Activate Apollo sequences for DC/MD licensed contractors
- [ ] Build GHL permit funnel + ops scorecard funnel
- [ ] Create Permit Checklist lead magnet PDF
- [ ] Join AGC-DC + DCBIA, attend first event
- [ ] Publish first 2 blog posts
- [ ] Set up LinkedIn posting schedule (3x/week minimum)

### Month 2: Scale

- [ ] Expand Google Ads to MD + VA jurisdictions
- [ ] Launch LinkedIn Ads (permit + ops campaigns)
- [ ] Start Google LSA registration
- [ ] Launch "The Permit Wire" newsletter #1
- [ ] Shoot first 2 YouTube videos
- [ ] Activate Facebook retargeting
- [ ] Reach out to 10 potential referral partners
- [ ] Import 2,000+ Apollo contacts, begin sequences

### Month 3: Optimize

- [ ] Review Google Ads performance, cut losers, double winners
- [ ] Launch paid social campaigns (Facebook/Instagram)
- [ ] List on Houzz + BuildingConnected
- [ ] Launch referral program with landing page
- [ ] Publish blog posts 3–4 (SEO begins compounding)
- [ ] Sponsor first association event
- [ ] Launch "Ops Insider" newsletter #1
- [ ] Analyze cost-per-lead by channel, reallocate budget to top performers

---

## Conversion Targets

| Metric | Month 1 | Month 3 | Month 6 | Month 12 |
|--------|---------|---------|---------|----------|
| Total leads | 100–150 | 300–500 | 500–790 | 800–1,200 |
| Discovery calls booked | 25–40 | 75–125 | 125–200 | 200–300 |
| Closed deals | 8–15 | 30–50 | 50–80 | 80–120 |
| Monthly revenue (permits + ops) | $8K–$15K | $30K–$55K | $55K–$95K | $95K–$160K |
| CAC (customer acquisition cost) | $150–$250 | $80–$120 | $60–$90 | $45–$70 |

*Revenue assumes mix of one-time products ($195–$2,995) and subscriptions ($295–$7,500/mo). CAC improves as organic channels (SEO, referrals, newsletter) compound.*
